thejournal_ie-2022.text:
Weekly Podcast
            The Explainer
            The Explainer is a weekly podcast from TheJournal.ie that takes a deeper look at one big news story you need to know about. Listen now wherever you get your podcasts

The42
                    
                    
                        The latest Irish and international sports news for readers and members

Noteworthy
                    
                    
                        A platform helping fund the type of in-depth journalism that the public wants to see
Tracker mortgage rates to rise after ECB hike interest rates by 0.5%
Convicted paedophile priest Tony Walsh sentenced to four more years for indecent assaults
AIB should 'reconsider' decision to make 70 branches cashless, says Taoiseach
Rishi Sunak says evidence suggests Tories would lose next election under Liz Truss
The Explainer: What do the recent extreme temperatures tell us about future Irish summers?

Ukraine grain deal expected tomorrow as Russian gas flows resume
81-year-old man to be sentenced for manslaughter of daughter

The Explainer: What do the recent extreme temperatures tell us about future Irish summers?

McDonald dismisses suggestion attendance at €2k 'Gold Table' Sydney dinner sends wrong message
Cost of living crisis may stop rapid inflation of secondhand car prices
The dressing room speech that helped Galway recover from late chaos against Armagh

Evening Wrap - 12 hours ago            
            The 5 at 5
            Five minutes, five stories, five o’clock…
AIB should 'reconsider' decision to make 70 branches cashless, says Taoiseach
Gardaí investigating death of a woman found with serious injuries in Limerick
Stormont to be recalled next week in latest attempt to elect speaker
Joe Biden tests positive for Covid-19
Guildford pub bomb planted by ‘courting couple’, coroner rules
Have you seen Anastasija? 38-year-old missing from Malahide in Dublin
O'Gara teaming up with Scott Robertson to coach Barbarians against All Blacks XV
Gardaí identify 61 new locations to park speed camera vans from next week
Free contraception for women aged 17 to 25 to be rolled out by September
'I should be on a flight home tonight': Shamrock Rovers fan released from Bulgarian jail
Eamon Ryan says 'we have to do more' as greenhouse gas emissions top pre-pandemic levels
'Impossible' targets would undermine agri-sector's 'green image', McConalogue told Ryan
Ireland 'urgently' needs to do more to protect coasts and adapt to climate change impacts
BBC agrees ‘substantial’ damages to the former nanny of Princes William and Harry
With the Taoiseach 9,000km away, Fianna Fáil backbenchers are not happy being brushed off
UK's actions on NI Protocol are 'not the way to behave with your neighbours' - Coveney
Munster to begin at home to Toulouse as provinces' European schedules are confirmed
Cultural sites in seven counties to seek Unesco World Heritage status
Opinion: In order to tackle the climate crisis, we must look to collective action
Italian prime minister Mario Draghi resigns after coalition implodes
Russian shelling kills at least two and wounds 19 in Kharkiv
Bloody Friday: Taoiseach pays tribute to victims on 50th anniversary of 'devastating' bombings
FactFind: How have other countries regulated Airbnb and what happened when they did?
Debunked: A drone image of lightning striking the Poolbeg towers has been digitally altered
FactCheck: Is the protocol stopping Jewish people in the North from practising their religion?
Poll: Who would you prefer to see as the next UK Prime Minister?
GP: Why are we waging a war on e-cigarettes when they help smokers quit?
Dr Garrett McGovern argues that banning e-cigarette flavours will increase smoking harm and death and will not protect young people.
Opinion: In order to tackle the climate crisis, we must look to collective action
Dr Patrick Bresnihan and Dr Fiadh Tubridy write that action on social problems should be considered a core part of the climate movement.
Money Diaries: An administration assistant on €31K living in Co Clare
This week, our reader enjoys cooking at home and cycling to keep fit while saving for a long-term plan of investing in a property.
Opinion: Is the Ukraine invasion finally whipping back the curtain to reveal the backstage grime of society?
Author Sam O’Brien explores what the current geopolitical situation tells us about what lies behind echelons of power.
Extract: I was in the prison when the call came through: 'We have a situation'
Former prison officer David McDonald has written a book about his career – in this extract, he writes about the beginning of a riot.
